notes: Sequential ID (EVT-0001...) assigned during extraction. Stable across re-runs if source data unchanged.
YYYY-MM-DD. Pre-war oil prices go back to 2026-01-02. Conflict events start 2026-02-28.

missing_code: Not available — only timeline events have timestamps
notes: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS. Times are approximate local time, not true UTC. Events span multiple time zones.
Day 0 = 2026-02-27 (pre-war baseline). Day 1 = 2026-02-28 (first strikes). Max = 30.

valid_values: CYBER | DIPLOMATIC | FINANCIAL | HUMANITARIAN | MILITARY | NAVAL | OTHER | RETALIATION | STRIKE
notes: Primary classification. STRIKE = US/Israeli offensive. RETALIATION = Iranian/proxy response. MILITARY = other military. NAVAL = maritime/fleet. FINANCIAL = economic data. DIPLOMATIC = political/diplomatic. HUMANITARIAN = casualties/damage. CYBER = cyber ops. OTHER = reference/summary.

valid_values: air | airstrike | alternative_route | amphibious | baseline_metric | carrier | cyber | daily_aggregate_snapshot | daily_briefing | daily_casualty_report | daily_war_cost | destroyer | diplomatic | domestic | economic | financial | ground | historical_comparison | hormuz_daily_narrative | humanitarian | infrastructure_damage | littoral_combat_ship | maritime_attack_summary | market_index | military | military_base | missile_attack | oil_price | operational_loss | political | rocket_attack | strait_closure | strategic_geography | submarine | summary | tanker_transit
notes: More specific than event_domain. 25 unique values. Key types: airstrike, missile_attack, rocket_attack, daily_casualty_report, oil_price, market_index, daily_war_cost, tanker_transit, baseline_metric, daily_aggregate_snapshot.
Free-text description. Format varies by source: timeline events = 'title: description'; strikes = 'city: target'; financial = metric summary.

valid_values: baselines.json | briefings/index.json | carriers.json | casualties.json | global-bases.json | hero-stats.json | historical-comparison.json | hormuz.json | infrastructure.json | markets.json | oil-prices.json | strikes-iran.json | strikes-retaliation.json | timeline-events.json | war-costs.json
notes: Which JSON file in public/data/ this row was extracted from.

missing_code: Not available — source has no record ID
notes: Original ID from source data (e.g., 'tehran-leadership', 'cvn-72'). NULL for timeline events, casualties, financial data.
City, base, or area name. Granularity varies: city-level for strikes, operational area for naval assets.
WGS84 decimal degrees. Precision varies: strike locations ~4 decimal places, naval assets approximate.
WGS84 decimal degrees.

valid_values: Bahrain | Cyprus | Diego Garcia | International Waters | Iran | Iraq | Israel | Jordan | Kuwait | Lebanon | Oman | Qatar | Saudi Arabia | UAE
notes: Extracted from city/location names via pattern matching. 'International Waters' for maritime events.
Who carried out the action. Values: US, Israel, US/Israel (joint), Hezbollah, Iran, IRGC Navy, various proxy groups, UK, France.
Who or what was targeted. For casualties, identifies the faction (e.g., 'Iran (military)', 'US (military)').

missing_code: Not applicable (non-retaliation event or not recorded)
notes: Weapon type from retaliation records. Examples: 'Ballistic missiles / cruise missiles / drones', 'Fattah hypersonic'.
Vessel name and hull number. Example: 'USS Abraham Lincoln (CVN-72)'.

Numeric casualty count when parseable, or text description. For daily casualty reports, this is the estimated daily killed for one faction. For strikes, attached to first target only to avoid double-counting.
Civilian killed count. Populated only for Iranian civilian daily casualty reports. These are daily estimates, not cumulative.
Military killed count. Populated for Iranian military, US military, and Israeli military daily reports. Daily estimates, not cumulative.

valid_values: air_defense | civilian | communications | healthcare | military_base | nuclear_facility | oil_infrastructure | power_grid | transportation
notes: Inferred from target descriptions. Values: military_base, civilian, nuclear_facility, oil_infrastructure, air_defense, communications, healthcare, power_grid, transportation.
Name of the financial indicator. Examples: 'Brent Crude', 'S&P 500', 'Daily US War Cost', 'Hormuz Tanker Transits'.
Numeric value of the financial metric. Units vary — see financial_metric_unit.

valid_values: USD millions | USD trillions | USD/bbl | USD/gallon | USD/oz | USD/share | index (Feb 27 = 100) | index points | million bbl/day | percent | transits/day
notes: Unit of measurement. Examples: 'USD/bbl', 'index (Feb 27 = 100)', 'USD millions', 'transits/day'.

missing_code: Not available — no escalation scoring in source data
notes: Reserved for future use. Not present in source data. Always NULL.
Assessment of data reliability. HIGH = from structured field with authoritative source. MEDIUM = inferred or from secondary sources. LOW = unverified, imputed, or from Wikipedia.
Source attribution from timeline record. Examples: 'DoD / White House', 'CENTCOM / IDF'.

missing_code: Not applicable or no data point recorded
notes: Quantitative highlight from the timeline event. Example: '3,000+ targets struck in first 72 hours'.
Detailed operational notes from source record. Often contains sourcing, context, and cross-references.
Whether the retaliation strike was verified in open-source reporting. True/False. 4 of 72 retaliation locations are unverified.
Strike group or command assignment. Example: 'Carrier Strike Group 3'.
Aircraft types embarked. Example: 'F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, F-35C Lightning II'.
Escort vessels listed, semicolon-separated.
Numeric count of damaged facilities. Parsed from text (e.g., '29+' → 29). Minimums; actual totals may be higher.

missing_code: Not available for this day (weekend/holiday or not reported)
notes: Brent crude price (USD/bbl) from daily aggregate snapshot. NULL on non-trading days.
WTI crude price (USD/bbl) from daily aggregate snapshot.
US national average gasoline price (USD/gallon). Reported intermittently.
S&P 500 index close. NULL on weekends and holidays.

variable_label: Daily Snapshot: Est. Daily US Cost
notes: Estimated daily US operational cost in millions USD. Based on Pentagon and CSIS anchor points with interpolation.
Cumulative estimated US cost in billions USD since Feb 28.
Cumulative US/Israeli targets struck. Reported at irregular intervals by CENTCOM/IDF.
Cumulative US military killed in action. NOTE: this is CUMULATIVE, unlike daily casualty reports which are daily estimates.
Cumulative US military wounded in action.
Cumulative Iranian killed. Figures from Red Crescent/HRANA — independently difficult to verify.
Cumulative Lebanese killed (all factions). Source: Lebanese Health Ministry.
Cumulative displaced persons across all affected countries.
Cumulative commercial flights cancelled due to conflict.
Cumulative children killed across Iran and Lebanon. Figures from HRANA/Red Crescent.
